facts.fact: The insurance policy number is 123 456 789.
The product is TD 20-Year Term Life insurance.
The insurance is underwritten by TD Life Insurance Company.
TD Life Insurance Company is a member of the TD Bank Group.
The insurer's mailing address is P.O. Box 1, TD Centre, Toronto, Ontario M5K 1A2.
The policy package is addressed to Jane Sample.
The insured person's address is 123 Address, City, Province, A1A 1A1.
The welcome letter is dated January 1, 2023.
The policy owner's first name is Jane.
The policy owner's last name is Sample.
The insured person's first name is Jane.
The insured person's last name is Sample.
The date of birth of the insured person is March 21, 1989.
The risk class is Female/Non-smoker.
The issue age is 34 years old.
The sum insured is $181,700.00.
The policy payor is Jane Sample.
The effective date is September 7, 2023.
The reinstatement effective date is September 7, 2023.
The first premium due date is October 7, 2023.
The plan is TD 20-Year Term Life insurance.
The waiver of premiums benefit is included (Yes).
The premium payment frequency is monthly.
The premium payment account type is Credit Card ending in 1234.
The basic premium from September 7, 2023 is $22.75.
The total monthly premium from September 7, 2023 is $20.00.
The basic premium from September 7, 2043 is $167.50.
The total monthly premium from September 7, 2043 is $150.75.
The basic premium from September 7, 2063 is $447.50.
The total monthly premium from September 7, 2063 is $447.50.
The basic premium from September 7, 2083 is $972.93.
The total monthly premium from September 7, 2083 is $972.93.
The premiums shown in the schedule are guaranteed not to increase while the policy is in effect.
The Waiver of Premium benefit ends at age 65.
The policy contract, along with application questions and answers and any other information submitted as evidence of insurability, forms the contract of insurance.
If kept in effect, the policy can provide coverage to March 21, 2103.
If the insured person dies while the policy is in effect, the insurer will pay the Sum Insured to the beneficiary or the estate if no beneficiary is named.
The policy is a non-participating policy.
All amounts are stated in Canadian Funds.
Taxes are included where applicable.
The policyholder has a 30-day review period from the effective date of the original policy.
If the policyholder decides to discontinue the policy during the 30-day review period, any premium paid will be promptly refunded.
If the policyholder cancels during the 30-day review period, the policy will be cancelled as of the effective date.
The cancellation during the 30-day period is only available if no claim has been made.
All obligations and liabilities under the policy end immediately when the insurer receives the request to cancel the policy.
To cancel the policy, the policyholder must provide a verbal or written request to TD Life Insurance Company, P.O. Box 1, TD Centre, Toronto, Ontario, M5K 1A2.
To cancel the policy, the policyholder may also contact TD Insurance at 1-888-788-0839.
Beneficiary means the person or persons named in writing to receive all benefits under the policy.
Effective Date means the date(s) the policy goes into effect, as shown in the Policy Schedule.
Grace Period is the length of time after the premium is due and unpaid during which the policy remains in effect.
If the full premium is paid during the grace period, the premium is considered to have been paid on time.
Lapse refers to the termination of the policy because the premium was not paid within the grace period.
Lapse Date refers to the date the policy lapses.
Insured person is the person whose life is insured under the policy.
Policy means the document which provides evidence of insurance.
Policy Owner is the person or party who owns the individual insurance policy.
The policy owner is not necessarily the person whose life is insured.
Reinstatement is the restoration of a lapsed policy.
Reinstatement Effective Date is the date the policy is reinstated.
Term Insurance is a type of insurance that provides protection for a limited number of years.

To be eligible for insurance coverage, a proposed insured person must be a Canadian citizen, or have applied for or been granted Permanent Resident (Landed Immigrant) status by the Canadian Government.
To be eligible for insurance coverage, a proposed insured person must be at least 18 years old and within 6 months of the 60th birthday at the time of application.
If a death benefit is payable, the insurer will pay the Sum Insured in effect at the time of death minus any outstanding premiums owed at the time the insured person dies.
The policy ends on the date the insured person dies.
While the policy is active, the policyholder may request an advance payment of 50% of the Death Benefit if the insured person has a terminal illness.
The maximum advance Living Benefit payment is $100,000.
To qualify for the Living Benefit, the insured person must be diagnosed with a terminal illness with a life expectancy of 12 months or less.
The policy must be in force and paid current for the Living Benefit to be payable.
The Living Benefit will be paid to the insured person.
Any irrevocable beneficiary or policy owner under the policy must consent to the payment of the Living Benefit.
A special collateral assignment arrangement is used to assign the policy to the insurer when the Living Benefit is approved and paid.
The Living Benefit is administered as a loan, which means in most instances the benefit is non-taxable.
When the Living Benefit is claimed, the policyholder may not cancel the policy.
If premiums are not paid after the Living Benefit is claimed (either directly or through the waiver benefit), the amount of any unpaid premiums will increase the amount of the living benefit.
The living benefit amount and any unpaid premium amount will accumulate interest until the death of the insured person.
Interest on the living benefit is set at the current prime rate and may be adjusted if prime fluctuates by 1% or more.
The insurer will not pay any insurance benefit if the claim for insurance benefits is not made within 365 days of the date of death of the insured person.
The insurer will not pay any insurance benefit if the insured person dies due to suicide within 2 years of the effective date of coverage or the most recent reinstatement effective date.
The suicide exclusion applies whether the insured person is aware or not aware of the result of their actions, regardless of their state of mind.

If no insurance benefit is payable as a result of suicide, the insurer will pay the policy owner 100% of premiums paid since the effective date or since the most recent reinstatement effective date if the policy has been reinstated.
The insurer will provide the benefits described in the policy if the policyholder pays the premiums shown in the premium schedule.
The policyholder must pay all premiums to the insurer by the premium due date.
If paying on a monthly basis, the first premium is due on the First Premium Due Date specified in the Policy Schedule.
Each subsequent monthly premium due date follows one calendar month after the previous.
If paying on an annual basis, the first premium is due on the First Premium Due Date specified in the Policy Schedule.
Each subsequent annual premium due date follows one year after the previous.
The policyholder may be eligible for a premium discount.
If the policyholder subsequently becomes ineligible for a premium discount, the insurer will adjust premiums accordingly.
Any premium collected that relates to insurance coverage after the date of death of the insured person will be refunded in the same manner as the Sum Insured.
The policyholder must notify the insurer if they wish to change the method or frequency of payments.
The insurer will allow a grace period of thirty (30) calendar days during which the policy will remain in effect even if premiums are not paid by the due date.
If a claim becomes payable during the grace period, all outstanding premiums will be deducted from any claim payable.
If outstanding premiums are not paid by the end of the grace period, the policy will lapse.
If the policy lapsed, the policyholder may apply to have it reinstated if the insured person is alive.
The policyholder may apply to reinstate the policy within 2 years of the lapse date.
To reinstate the policy, the insured person must be alive.
To reinstate the policy, the policyholder must pay all unpaid premiums due from the lapse date up to and including the reinstatement effective date.
To reinstate the policy, the policyholder must provide a completed Reinstatement Application.
To reinstate the policy, the policyholder must provide new evidence of insurability for the insured person satisfactory to the insurer.
The policyholder may convert the insurance to permanent life insurance at any time up to and including the policy anniversary nearest to the insured person's 69th birthday.
Conversion to permanent life insurance requires no medical examination.
The converted insurance will be for an amount not to exceed the Sum Insured provided on the Policy Schedule.
The converted insurance will be on any permanent plan the insurer makes available for such conversion.
To exercise the conversion option, the policyholder must complete an application form and pay the first premium required for the converted insurance.

The premium for the converted insurance depends on the age of the insured person at the time the conversion option is exercised, the risk classification used for the original policy, and the plan of the converted insurance.
The converted policy will not include any waiver of premium benefits that existed on the original policy.
Before the insurer will pay any claim, the claimant must provide proof, satisfactory to the insurer, that the insured person has died.
The claimant must provide, upon the insurer's request, access to any medical records required to assess the claim, including an attending physician's statement and a coroner's report.
Proof of claim must be provided within one (1) year of the date of death of the insured person.
Any cost incurred in providing proof of claim is at the claimant's expense.
All coverage under the policy will end on the earliest date when the insured person dies.
All coverage under the policy will end when the insurer receives a request from the policyholder to cancel coverage.
All coverage under the policy will end at the end of the grace period if premiums are not paid.
All coverage under the policy will end upon a fraudulent claim made by the policyholder or the beneficiary.
All coverage under the policy will end on the insured person's 80th birthday.
The policy will renew automatically at the end of the initial 20-year period for successive 20-year renewal periods.
The final renewal period will only run until the insured person's 80th birthday.
At the insured person's 80th birthday, the policy will end and there will be no further renewal.
If a benefit is payable, it will be paid to the beneficiary(ies).
If there is no beneficiary when the insured person dies, the benefit will be paid to the policy owner or the policy owner's estate.
The right to name or change the beneficiary designation is reserved to the policy owner only.
The policy owner does not need the consent of any beneficiary to change any beneficiary under the policy, unless any rule of law requires consent.
All beneficiary designations are revocable unless otherwise stated or unless any contrary law applies.
To name or change a beneficiary, the policy owner must submit a written request to the insurer.
A beneficiary change shall not be binding on the insurer until received at the insurer's Head Office.
Once a beneficiary change request is received, it will be the insurer's responsibility to fulfill the request as of the date the request was signed.
The insurer will not be responsible for any benefits paid to the previous beneficiary before the receipt of the beneficiary request.
The insurer is not bound by any documents that affect rights under the contract unless the document itself or written notice of it is received at the insurer's Head Office.
The insurer is not responsible for ensuring that any such document is valid or has the desired effect.

If incorrect information was provided during the application process that qualified the insured person for preferred rates, the insurer reserves the right to re-issue the policy with an adjusted premium based on the correct information.
Any information provided after the policy's effective date that impacts the premium can only be honored at time of notification.

If a policy is issued based on an incorrect age and the insured person is still eligible for insurance, the premium amount will be adjusted to the correct amount based on the correct date of birth at the insured person's effective date.
If premiums were overpaid due to incorrect age, the insurer will refund the excess premiums calculated at the time a claim is made against the policy.
If premiums were underpaid due to incorrect age, the insurer will decrease the benefit amount by the amount underpaid at the time a claim is made against the policy.

If the insured person is not eligible for insurance due to an incorrect age, all coverages under the policy will be considered never to have been in force and the insurer will refund all premiums paid.

If a policy is issued based on an incorrect sex assigned at birth, the premium amount will be adjusted to the correct amount based on the correct sex assigned at birth at the insured person's effective date.
If premiums were overpaid due to incorrect sex, the insurer will refund the excess premiums calculated at the time a claim is made.
If premiums were underpaid due to incorrect sex, the insurer will decrease the benefit amount by the amount underpaid at the time a claim is made.
The insurer relies on the truth and completeness of the statements and answers given as evidence of insurability.
The insurer may void insurance coverage if the policyholder has concealed or misrepresented any information.

After insurance has been in effect for two (2) years from the effective date or reinstatement effective date, the insurer will treat all answers given as evidence of insurability as true, except for cases of fraudulent misrepresentation.
The two-year contestability provision does not apply to information about the date of birth of the insured person.

The insurer shall not be deemed to have waived or changed any condition of the policy unless such waiver or change is clearly expressed in writing and signed by an officer of TD Life Insurance Company.
No action at law or in equity shall be brought to recover on the policy prior to the expiration of 120 days after proof of claim has been furnished in accordance with the requirements of the policy.
Every action or proceeding against an insurer for the recovery of insurance money payable under the contract is absolutely barred unless commenced within the time set out in applicable legislation.
For actions governed by the laws of Alberta and British Columbia, the time limit is set out in the Insurance Act.
For actions governed by the laws of Manitoba, the time limit is set out in the Insurance Act.
For actions governed by the laws of Ontario, the time limit is set out in the Limitations Act, 2002.
For actions governed by the laws of Quebec, the prescriptive period is set out in the Civil Code of Quebec.

The insurance will be governed by the laws of the province where the policyholder was resident when they initially applied for insurance, without reference to that province's rules about conflicts of laws.
It is the express wish of the parties that the policy and all related documents, including notices and other communications, be drawn up in the English language only.
The Waiver of Premiums Benefit section is applicable only if the policyholder selected the Waiver of Premiums Benefit when applying for TD 20-Year Term Life Insurance.

If the insured person becomes totally disabled before the insured person's 65th birthday and remains totally disabled for a continuous period of six months, the insurer will waive the payment of monthly premiums.

Upon qualifying for waiver, the insurer will automatically change the policy premium payment frequency to monthly if the payment frequency is other than monthly, beginning with the date the insurer agrees to waive premiums.

The insurer will waive the payment of monthly premiums from the date the insured person becomes totally disabled and for as long as the total disability continues, up to the insured person's 80th birthday.

Total disability or totally disabled means the insured person, as a result of bodily injury or disease, is wholly prevented during the first two years after the injury or onset of the disease from performing the substantial duties of their own occupation.

After the first two years of total disability, the insured person must be prevented by the injury or disease from engaging in any gainful occupation for which they are or may reasonably become qualified by training, education or experience.
If the conversion option is exercised while the insured person is totally disabled, the insurer will not waive premiums for the converted insurance policy.
The insurer will not waive premiums if total disability results directly or indirectly from attempted suicide or self-inflicted injury.

The insurer will not waive premiums if total disability results directly or indirectly from war, whether declared or undeclared, any act of war or activities directly related to service in the armed forces of any country.
The insurer will not waive premiums if total disability results directly or indirectly from the commission or attempted commission by the insured person of a criminal act.

The insurer will not waive premiums if total disability results directly or indirectly from an accident that occurs while the insured person is operating any kind of conveyance while their blood alcohol level exceeds the legal limit where the accident occurs.
The insurer will not waive premiums if total disability results directly or indirectly from taking a drug in a manner other than prescribed by a licensed physician.
The insurer must receive notice of total disability while the insured person is alive and continues to be totally disabled.
The insurer must receive proof of total disability of the insured person within 3 months of the date notice was provided.
The insurer will not waive any premiums falling due more than 12 months before the insured person gives notice of total disability.
At any time, the insurer may ask for proof that the insured person continues to be totally disabled.

If proof of continued total disability is not provided, the insured person will be deemed to no longer be totally disabled on the day prior to the date on which the insurer asked for the proof, and monthly premiums will begin again.

If after the insurer has waived at least one premium the insured person recovers, but within 6 months again becomes totally disabled from the same or a related cause, the subsequent period of total disability will be considered a continuation of the former period.
The premiums for the waiver of premiums benefit are payable until the insured person's 65th birthday.
The premiums for the waiver of premiums benefit are shown in the Policy Schedule.
The agents of TD Insurance hold life and health insurance licenses in Canada.
TD Insurance agents promote life and health insurance products of TD Life Insurance Company.
TD Insurance agents are sponsored by TD Life and employed by TD Assurance Agency Inc.
TD Assurance Agency holds contracts with TD Life.
TD Assurance Agency is a member of TD Bank Financial Group.
TD Insurance agents are salaried employees of TD Bank Financial Group.
TD Insurance agents do not receive commission with respect to the sale of insurance.
TD Insurance agents may be eligible for an annual bonus based on their overall performance.
TD Insurance agents are prohibited from having personal conflicts of interest in regards to the sales transaction.
The agent is required to make a recommendation based solely on a review of the Needs Assessment Tool that identified a gap in insurance coverage.
The policyholder declares they will inspect the policy if issued to verify its terms are satisfactory.
The concealment, misrepresentation or false declaration in the application could void the insurance policy if issued.
Payment of any benefits is subject to the limitations and exclusions as described in the policy.
No insurance coverage under the policy will take effect until the earliest of TD Life's written approval or the effective date of the policy if issued.
The purchase of this insurance is voluntary and is not required to obtain any other product or services from TD Life or its affiliates.
The authorization shall be valid as long as the policyholder remains a client of TD Life.

By applying for TD 20-Year Term Life insurance, the policyholder agrees that the companies that insure and reinsure coverage may collect, use and disclose information as described in the Privacy Agreement.

The policyholder authorizes any licensed physician, medical practitioner, hospital, clinic, other medical or medically related facility, insurance company, MIB, Inc., or other organization to release health records to TD Life or its reinsurers.
TD Life, or its authorized administrators or reinsurers, may make a brief report of personal health information to the MIB.
MIB is a non-profit membership organization of life insurance companies that operates an information exchange on behalf of its members.
In the event of a claim, the policyholder's heirs and the executor or administrator of the estate are authorized to provide TD Life with all information and authorizations needed for claims purposes.
The policyholder consents to TD Insurance's Privacy Policy.
TD Insurance includes the Toronto Dominion Bank and affiliated companies (collectively 'TD').
The Privacy Policy is available online at td.com/privacy.
TD collects, uses, shares and retains information to identify the policyholder.
TD collects, uses, shares and retains information to process the application and assess eligibility.
TD collects, uses, shares and retains information to underwrite insurance.
TD collects, uses, shares and retains information to provide ongoing service.
TD collects, uses, shares and retains information to communicate with the policyholder.
TD collects, uses, shares and retains information to personalize the relationship.
TD collects, uses, shares and retains information to determine the right product, premium or coverage.
TD collects, uses, shares and retains information to improve TD products and services.
TD collects, uses, shares and retains information to protect against fraud, financial abuse and error.
TD collects, uses, shares and retains information to manage and assess its risks.
TD collects, uses, shares and retains information to meet legal and regulatory obligations.
TD collects information from fraud prevention agencies and registries.

TD collects information from any health care professional, medically-related facility, insurance company, government agency, organizations who manage public information data banks, or insurance information bureaus including MIB, LLC and the Insurance Bureau of Canada, that have knowledge of the policyholder's information.
TD collects information from interactions with the policyholder, including on mobile devices or the Internet, cameras at TD's property and records of use of TD's products and services.
TD collects information from a personal investigation report prepared in verifying and/or authenticating information provided in the life or health insurance application.
TD may share information with TD affiliates.
TD may share information with fraud prevention agencies and registries.
TD may share information with health-care professionals.
TD may share information with companies that TD works with to provide products or services.
TD may share information with insurance companies including prospective insurers and reinsurers.
TD may share information with organizations who manage public information data banks, or insurance information bureaus, including the MIB, LLC and the Insurance Bureau of Canada.
Some parties with whom TD shares information may be located outside the policyholder's province/territory or outside Canada.
TD keeps policyholder information for as long as it is reasonably needed for the stated purposes.
TD may communicate with the policyholder about their application and about other products and services that may be of interest.
TD may contact the policyholder by phone or text at the number(s) provided, or by mail, email or other electronic methods.
The policyholder can opt out of receiving offers or choose how TD contacts them for marketing purposes by contacting TD at 1-888-788-0839.
TD may share the policyholder's non-health personal information with affiliates to offer products and services by telephone, internet, mail or other methods.
The policyholder's response to the question of whether sharing non-health personal information with affiliates is okay was Yes.
The policyholder's response to agreeing to the terms and conditions of the authorization was Yes.
The policy renews automatically for successive 20-year terms as long as premiums are paid, providing continuous coverage until the insured person's 80th birthday.
Premiums are fixed for each 20-year term.
Premiums are guaranteed not to increase until each renewal date, regardless of age or changes in health.
Coverage can be converted from term life insurance to permanent life insurance at any time up to and including the policy anniversary nearest the insured's 69th birthday.
Conversion from term to permanent life insurance requires no medical examination.
The TD 20-Year Term Life insurance coverage is also available to the policyholder's spouse, partner or other family members.
Applying for coverage for a spouse or family member can be done at www.tdinsurance.com or by calling the number provided.
TD Insurance representatives can be reached toll-free at 1-888-788-0839, Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 10 p.m. (ET) and Saturday 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. (ET).
The first monthly premium was scheduled to be deducted from the payment account on January 1, 2023.
Subsequent monthly premiums are deducted thereafter on the first of every month.
Benefits from the policy paid on death are tax-free to the designated beneficiary under current tax law.
The Waiver of Premium Benefit must be added at the same time as the purchase of TD 20-Year Term Life insurance.
The Beneficiary Designation Form is on the last page of the policy booklet.
The completed Beneficiary Designation Form should be returned in the enclosed postage-paid envelope.
If no beneficiary is named, the benefits will be paid to the policyholder's estate.
The policyholder can change the beneficiary designation by submitting a written request to the insurer.
The policy letter was signed by Erika Schiavoni, Vice President, Product and Pricing, Life, Health and Credit Protection, TD Life Insurance Company.
TD Life Insurance Company is the authorized administrator for the insurance.
Beneficiaries can be designated as Revocable or Irrevocable.
If a beneficiary is designated as irrevocable, they must consent to certain changes the policyholder may want to make to the policy in the future.
An irrevocable beneficiary must consent to any request to change the beneficiary or surrender the policy.
In Quebec, the spouse is automatically deemed to be an irrevocable beneficiary unless specifically designated as revocable.
To change an irrevocable minor beneficiary designation in the future, the minor child must have reached the age of majority (18 or older, based on the jurisdiction).
Until a minor child reaches the age of majority, the law does not permit the legal guardian or child to sign release of the irrevocable designation.
A Trustee does not have the entitlement prior to the death of the insured person to sign a release of irrevocable minor beneficiary designation.
To revoke a minor child irrevocable beneficiary designation, a court order will be required.
Proceeds payable to a named beneficiary (someone other than 'estate') are paid directly to the beneficiary and do not flow through the estate.
No probate or executor fees will be deducted from proceeds payable to a named beneficiary.
No estate creditors can make claims against proceeds payable to a named beneficiary.
Proceeds are available to the beneficiary as soon as the claim is approved since they do not become part of the estate.
Proceeds payable to 'Estate' are distributed, in the absence of a will, according to the intestacy laws of the policyholder's province.
A Contingent Beneficiary Designation is a 'secondary' list of beneficiary(ies) who will receive the proceeds if none of the primary beneficiaries are living at the time of death.
An Irrevocable Beneficiary Designation means the policyholder gives up the right to change the beneficiary designation unless the irrevocable beneficiary consents.
A Minor is a person who has not attained the age of majority and, thus, has limited contractual capacity.
A Primary Beneficiary Designation is a list of beneficiary(ies) who will receive the proceeds of the insurance in the event of the policyholder's death.

A Revocable Beneficiary Designation means the beneficiary has no right to the policy proceeds during the insured's lifetime because the owner has the unrestricted right to change the beneficiary designation at any time.
A Trustee holds legal title to property known as the trust fund for the benefit of another person.
The law does not allow an insurer to pay benefits directly to minors.
TD Life cannot warrant the legal effectiveness of any change of beneficiary.
The Change of Beneficiary, after it has been recorded by TD Life, relates back to and takes effect as of the date the request is signed, or the date of receipt by TD Life, whichever is later.
Any payment made by TD Life prior to the receipt of a beneficiary change shall be without prejudice to TD Life.
The beneficiary designation form must be witnessed by someone who is not related to the policyholder and who is also not named as a beneficiary.
For the beneficiary designation form, if the owner of the coverage is a corporation, a corporate seal is required to accompany the signature of the signing officer.
If a corporate seal is not available, the insurer will accept the signatures of 2 signing officers with their titles.
TD Insurance representatives can be reached for questions about the beneficiary form at 1-888-788-0839.
The Home and Auto Insurance customer service number is 1-888-588-5999, Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 8 p.m., and Saturday 9 a.m. to 4 p.m., Eastern Time.
The Travel Insurance customer service number is 1-866-368-6509, Monday to Saturday, 8 a.m. to 9 p.m., Eastern Time.
Per the introductory letter, the insurance took effect on January 1, 2023.
A reproduction of the authorization will be as valid as the original.

The Waiver of Premiums Benefit suicide/self-inflicted injury exception applies whether the insured person is aware or not aware of the result of their actions, regardless of the insured person's state of mind.
The document is marked as a SAMPLE policy package.
The policy booklet cover states: 'Your TD 20-Year Term Life insurance policy # 123 456 789 is enclosed in this booklet'.
TD Life Insurance Company is referred to as 'the insurer' and 'we', 'us' and 'our' throughout the policy.
Any applicable Endorsements to the policy will be mailed to the policyholder separately and will form part of the policy.
The policyholder is asked to review their Health Question Confirmation responses for accuracy and retain for their records.
The policyholder is asked to review their Privacy Agreement for accuracy and retain for their records.
If any answers in the Health Question Confirmation or Privacy Agreement are incorrect or incomplete, the policyholder should contact the insurer immediately at 1-888-788-0839.
Incorrect or incomplete answers could affect the policyholder's insurability.
During the 30-day review period, the policyholder will be insured as outlined in the policy.
If not completely satisfied during the 30-day review period, the policyholder should contact the insurer within the 30-day period.
Any premium charged to the account will be promptly refunded in full if the policyholder cancels within the 30-day review period.
The Premiums Summary shows the Waiver of Premiums column as N/A for all four scheduled premium periods.
The Premiums Summary has four scheduled premium rate periods (starting September 7, 2023; September 7, 2043; September 7, 2063; September 7, 2083) followed by four rows all showing N/A.
The payment of the Living Benefit is subject to the insurer's administrative rules in effect at the time of the request.
After the end of a period of total disability, if the policyholder wishes to pay premiums other than monthly, they must notify the insurer.
The process of putting a lapsed policy back into effect is called reinstatement.
The Waiver of Premiums exceptions apply whether total disability results either directly or indirectly from the listed causes.
The recurrent total disability provision applies only after the insurer has waived at least one premium under the Waiver of Premiums benefit.
The Agent Disclosure information was provided by agents in a telephone conversation with the policyholder.
Should the policyholder require additional information about the agents' qualifications or the nature of their business relationships, the insurer would be pleased to provide it.
If the policyholder decides to cancel coverage after the 30-day review period, and provided there are no claims pending, the insurer will refund any unearned premiums.
To cancel coverage after the 30-day period, the policyholder should call the insurer.
The policyholder's answers form part of the application along with any supplementary applications or forms that TD Life may require be submitted.
The policyholder has been provided with the opportunity to review all of their responses in the application.
The premium is set and guaranteed for each 20-year term of insurance coverage.
The premium will not change during each 20-year term regardless of changes in age or health, provided premium payments are made when due and the policy does not lapse.
No re-application is needed at the end of each 20-year term; coverage automatically renews with no medical questions required.
Coverage cannot be renewed beyond the insured person's 80th birthday.
The benefit amount will be payable to the designated beneficiary(ies), which could be the policyholder's spouse, child, friend or any other person selected.
If no beneficiary(ies) are designated, the benefit amount will be payable to the policy owner or policy owner's estate as applicable.
The policyholder cannot be turned down for coverage under the conversion option regardless of health.
Premiums on the converted policy will be based on the insured's age at the time of conversion.
Under current tax law, benefits paid on the insurance are tax-free to the designated beneficiary.
The Waiver of Premium Benefit is available to the policy owner for an additional cost.
The Waiver of Premium Benefit can ensure that coverage remains in place should the policyholder become disabled and cannot return to work.
TD Insurance offers the Critical Illness Recovery Plan.
TD Insurance offers the Critical Accident Recovery Plan.
TD Insurance offers an array of insurance solutions.

For details on TD Insurance Critical Illness/Accident products, the policyholder can visit tdinsurance.com or call 1-888-788-0839, Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 10 p.m., and Saturday 10 a.m. to 6 p.m., Eastern Time.
Death of a spouse or partner could cause financial hardship such as childcare expenses, medical bills, and funeral expenses.
TD 20-Year Term Life insurance premiums will increase at each renewal of a 20-year term.
Coverage renews automatically every 20 years until the insured person turns 80 years old, as long as premiums are paid, unless the policyholder chooses not to continue coverage.
There is no medical examination required for automatic renewals of the TD 20-Year Term Life insurance.
The coverage could provide a tax-free lump sum benefit to help cover tuition costs to benefit children's future.
The coverage could provide a tax-free lump sum benefit to help pay down a mortgage so the family can remain in the family home.
The coverage could provide a tax-free lump sum benefit to help ensure funeral expenses won't become a burden.
The coverage could provide a tax-free lump sum benefit to provide cash for day-to-day living expenses.
The coverage could provide a tax-free lump sum benefit to provide extra money to pay medical bills or outstanding debts.
TD Insurance Life and Health products information can be obtained by calling 1-888-788-0839, Monday to Friday 8 a.m. to 10 p.m., and Saturday 10 a.m. to 6 p.m., Eastern Time.
Home and auto insurance helps provide the resources needed should a fire, accident or break-in happen in the policyholder's home or car.
Travel Medical Insurance protects the policyholder and their family from costly medical bills when traveling out-of-province and out-of-country.
Travel insurance options include per trip, annual multi trip coverages, trip cancellation and interruption insurance.
The beneficiary designation form instructions require the policyholder to type or print all information using a ball point pen.
The beneficiary designation form instructions prohibit the use of correction fluid (liquid paper).
The beneficiary designation form instructions require the policyholder to initial any corrections/changes.
Use of all lines on the beneficiary designation form is not required.
If necessary, the policyholder can use a separate piece of paper to list all beneficiary designations.
For primary beneficiaries on the form, the policyholder must indicate the First, Middle and Last Name, Date of Birth or Age, Percent of Benefit, and whether proceeds will be Revocable or Irrevocable.

For a contingent beneficiary on the form, after the statement 'if living, otherwise to', the policyholder must indicate the First, Middle and Last Name, Date of Birth or Age, Percent of Benefit, and whether proceeds will be Revocable or Irrevocable.
If the policyholder wishes to designate a child/children as Primary or Contingent Beneficiary, the insurer recommends that the policyholder also name a Trustee.

For a trustee for minor children, the policyholder should refer to page 2 of the beneficiary form and indicate the First, Middle and Last Name, Date of Birth or Age, Percent of Benefit, and whether proceeds will be Revocable or Irrevocable.
Beneficiary form Step 2 requires mailing the 'Request for Change of Beneficiary' to TD Life in the enclosed self-addressed return envelope.
TD Life will validate and record the Change of Beneficiary and return a confirmation letter to the policyholder for their records.
Settlement of the estate usually takes a few months but can take years depending on the circumstances.
The Contingent Beneficiary Designation is described as 'subrogated in Quebec'.
The Request for Change of Beneficiary form references policy number 123 456 789.
The Request for Change of Beneficiary form names insured person Jane Sample.
Section 1a of the beneficiary form covers benefits to be paid at the death of the insured Jane Sample.
Primary beneficiary percentages on the form (items a through g) must total 100%.
For Quebec only, on the beneficiary form, the designation of a spouse as a beneficiary is deemed to be irrevocable unless specified as revocable.
Section 1b of the beneficiary form covers the contingent beneficiaries to be paid if beneficiaries named in section 1a are not living.
Contingent beneficiary percentages (a+b+c) must equal (d) 100%.
The Trustee section on the beneficiary form is for minor children and is not applicable in Quebec, as the proceeds will be paid in trust to the minor child's tutor.
Trustee percentages on the beneficiary form (a+b) must equal (c) 100%.
The beneficiary designation form contains 5 signature lines.
Signature line 1 on the beneficiary form is for the Signature of Owner.
Signature line 2 on the beneficiary form is for the Signature of Irrevocable Beneficiary (if applicable).
Signature line 3 on the beneficiary form is for the Signature and Title of Owner (if a Corporation).
Signature line 4 on the beneficiary form is for the Signature and Title of Owner (if a Corporation).
Signature line 5 on the beneficiary form is for the Signature of Witness.

Section 2 of the beneficiary form states that at the death of any other Insured Person, benefits will be paid to the Insured named above, if living, otherwise as though it were a sum payable under section 1 above.
Where the beneficiary is a Trust, TD Life is relieved of all responsibility upon making payment to such Trust.
TD Life need not inquire into the Trustee's use of funds nor the scope of the Trustee's authority when the beneficiary is a Trust.
The beneficiary change, after being recorded by TD Life, relates back to and takes effect as of the date the request is signed, or the date of receipt by TD Life, whichever is later.
The beneficiary form contains a 'FOR OFFICE USE ONLY' section.
The beneficiary form contains a section for placing a Corporate Seal if the owner of this coverage is a Corporation.
The beneficiary form contains a Validation field and Date Signed field in the Office Use Only section.
The Agent Disclosure section was provided to confirm disclosures made by agents in their telephone conversation with the policyholder.

The policyholder has the option to refuse or withdraw their consent as outlined in the Privacy Policy.
The cancellation request during the 30-day review period can be made by calling 1-888-788-0839 or by submitting a written request.
The renewal periods are each of the same length as the initial 20-year period, except the renewal anniversary nearest to the insured person's 80th birthday.
The claimant must provide further proof of claim, if applicable, in addition to proof of death and medical records.
The conversion option is available regardless of the health of the insured person.
The policy instructs the policyholder to please read the policy carefully and keep it as their permanent record of coverage.
TD Life Insurance Company is referred to as 'TD Life' in the Declaration and Authorization section.
The policyholder may choose not to be contacted regarding direct marketing offers by informing TD Life.
All trade-marks are the property of their respective owners.
The TD logo and other TD trade-marks are the property of The Toronto-Dominion Bank.
The policy uses italics throughout the document to denote defined terms.
The beneficiary designation form for section 1a lists up to 7 primary beneficiary slots (a through g).
The beneficiary form requires each signature to be accompanied by a date signed in MM/DD/YR format.
If a previously designated irrevocable beneficiary exists, they must also sign the beneficiary change form on signature line 2.
The beneficiary change form states that it requests all prior beneficiary designations under the policy be revoked and replaced by the new designations.
The Contingent Beneficiary section on the beneficiary form lists up to 3 contingent beneficiary slots (a, b, c).
The Trustee section on the beneficiary form lists up to 2 trustee slots (a, b).
The insurer describes the TD 20-Year Term Life insurance as 'convenient and straightforward coverage'.
The policy states that by choosing TD 20-Year Term Life insurance, the policyholder has taken an important step in helping to secure the financial future of those they love if they were to die.
The insurer states the TD 20-Year Term Life insurance could provide a valuable tax-free benefit for the policyholder's family when they need it most.
The policy instructs the policyholder to keep the policy in a safe place with their other important documents.
The insurer's letter instructs the policyholder to keep the policy in a safe place with their valuable papers.
The Irrevocable Beneficiary Designation will also affect any other desired changes the policyholder may want to make to the policy in the future.

Relationship examples for beneficiary designations include: Spouse, child, mother, father, brother, sister, aunt, uncle, cousin, niece, nephew, grandmother, grandfather, sister-in-law, brother-in-law, mother-in-law, father-in-law, friend, estate.
The policy owner is the only person entitled to change the beneficiary, whether the coverage is for themselves or for their entire family.
The policy owner must obtain consent of any previously designated irrevocable beneficiaries when changing the beneficiary.
The Premiums Summary form is signed by Erika Schiavoni, Vice President, Product and Pricing, Life, Health and Credit Protection, TD Life Insurance Company.
The authorization section confirms that insuring companies may collect, use and disclose information to help identify the policyholder, provide ongoing service, and meet legal requirements.
The authorization in the Declaration and Authorization section is given by the policyholder on behalf of their heirs and the executor or administrator of their estate for claims purposes.
The insurer uses 'you' and 'your' to refer to the policy owner throughout the policy.
The total disability definition specifies the disability must result from bodily injury or disease.
The introductory letter states that the policyholder has joined thousands of TD customers who feel confident they have made a wise financial decision for themselves and the people who depend on them.
The insurer states that life insurance is the cornerstone of a sound financial plan.
The introductory letter states the insurer appreciates the policyholder's business and looks forward to serving the policyholder and their family.
The policy booklet contains a Notes section spanning two pages (pages 31 and 32) with blank lines for the policyholder's use.
The policy document states 'This is the end of the policy' at the conclusion of the Waiver of Premiums Benefit section.
The Living Benefit conditions include a requirement that the insured person be diagnosed with a terminal illness; this condition must be met in addition to the policy being in force and paid current.

The eligibility requirement states that the proposed insured must be a Canadian citizen OR have applied for/been granted Permanent Resident status, AND must be at least 18 years old and within 6 months of the 60th birthday.
The insurer confirms that in most instances the Living Benefit administered as a loan is non-taxable.
The policy states it is issued to Jane Sample at 123 Address, City, Province, A1A 1A1.
The Reinstatement Effective Date is described in the section 'Putting your policy back into effect'.
The Reinstatement definition cross-references the section 'Putting your policy back into effect'.

The policy confirms that TD Life Insurance Company is the authorized administrator for the insurance, and refers the policyholder to the Insurance Policy for more details on insurer and/or administrator information.

Today with medical breakthroughs, more and more Canadians are surviving critical illnesses (cancer, heart attack or stroke) and injuries from critical accidents, creating the need for additional coverage beyond life insurance.
